description_long: This is a classic cast iron radiator mounted against a flat wall panel, featuring seven vertical, rounded columns with a ribbed design for efficient heat dissipation. The radiator is supported by four short feet and is connected to the wall by two visible pipe connections, one at the top right and one at the bottom right, each fitted with a manual valve. The surface is smooth and glossy, with a metallic gray finish that reflects light subtly. The radiator's fins are arranged in evenly spaced vertical columns, maximizing surface area for heat transfer. There is no decorative grille or cover; the design is utilitarian and functional, with all mechanical parts exposed. The overall appearance is robust and industrial, suitable for urban interiors or utility spaces.
description: A metallic gray, wall-mounted cast iron radiator with seven vertical columns and exposed manual valves, designed for efficient heat emission. Its smooth, glossy surface and utilitarian style make it suitable for urban interiors.
description_view_0: Front view showing seven vertical columns, two pipe connections with manual valves on the right, and the radiator's ribbed design.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the radiator's depth, the rounded profile of the columns, and the proximity to the wall panel.
description_view_2: Top-down view showing the flat wall panel and the alignment of the radiator's columns, with no visible pipework from this angle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the radiator's depth and the rounded ends of the columns.
attribute_pipe_connections: two, right side, top and bottom, horizontal orientation
